description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline that uses the ruby:3.2 Docker image and defines two stages: build and deploy. In the build stage add a job called build_site that caches the Bundler directory at vendor/bundle, runs `gem install bundler`, then `bundle install --path vendor/bundle`, and finally `bundle exec jekyll build -d public` to generate the static site, and publishes the `public/` folder as an artifact available always. In the deploy stage add a pages job that simply passes the `public/` artifact to GitLab Pages and is restricted to run only on the main branch. Set the variable JEKYLL_ENV=production for the build job.
